data.HEADLINE: Summer Hydration: Beyond Just Water
data.BODY: In summer heat, you can lose 2-3 liters of sweat per hour during intense exercise. Dehydration of just 2% body weight reduces performance by up to 25%. Plan your fluids like you plan your meals.
data.STAT_LABEL: Sweat loss per hour in summer heat
data.TIP_TEXT: Wake up: 16oz water. Pre-workout: 16oz 30min before. During: 6-8oz every 15min. Post: 24oz per pound lost. NourishAI's water tracker sends reminders throughout the day.

data.HEADLINE: Fueling Outdoor Summer Workouts
data.BODY: Training in heat increases calorie burn by 7-10% but also increases nutrient demands. You need more sodium, more carbs for fuel, and better pre-cooling strategies.
data.STAT_LABEL: More calories burned in hot weather training
data.TIP_TEXT: Train early morning or evening. Eat a carb-rich meal 2 hours before. Freeze a sports drink for sipping during. Post-workout: watermelon + salt is nature's recovery drink.

data.HEADLINE: Manual Entry: Log Anything in Seconds
data.SUBTITLE: Type a food name and NourishAI fills in the macros automatically
data.FEATURE1_DESC: Type 'chicken breast' and get instant nutrition data from our food database
data.FEATURE2_DESC: Adjust serving size with a slider — macros update in real time
data.FEATURE3_DESC: One-tap logging for your go-to meals and frequent foods

data.HEADLINE: Vacation Nutrition: Stay on Track While Traveling
data.BODY: Summer travel doesn't mean abandoning your goals. Focus on protein at every meal, stay hydrated on flights, and use NourishAI's photo scan at restaurants — no calorie counting needed.
data.STAT_LABEL: Extra calories typically consumed per travel day
data.TIP_TEXT: Pack: protein bars, mixed nuts, dried fruit, individual nut butter packets. At airports: grilled chicken salads, hard-boiled eggs, fruit cups. Avoid: oversized muffins and sugary coffee drinks.

data.HEADLINE: Couples Who Track Together, Win Together
data.BEFORE_STAT2: One partner always sabotaging the other
data.AFTER_STAT3: Lost a combined 28 lbs in 3 months

data.HEADLINE: Vitamin D: The Sunshine Vitamin
data.BODY: Despite more sun in summer, 42% of Americans are still Vitamin D deficient. This vitamin is critical for bone health, immune function, mood, and muscle strength.
data.STAT_LABEL: of Americans are Vitamin D deficient
data.TIP_TEXT: 15-20 min of midday sun (arms and legs exposed). Eat: fatty fish, egg yolks, fortified milk. Supplement 2000-4000 IU daily if levels are low. Ask your doctor to test.

data.HEADLINE: 5 Smoothie Mistakes Killing Your Macros
data.BODY: Smoothies can be a 200-calorie power breakfast or a hidden 800-calorie sugar bomb. The difference is knowing what to add — and what to skip.
data.STAT_LABEL: Calories in an average juice bar smoothie
data.TIP_TEXT: Base: water or unsweetened almond milk (not juice). Protein: 1 scoop whey or pea protein. Fruit: 1/2 cup berries (not banana + mango + pineapple). Fat: 1 tbsp nut butter. Green: handful of spinach.

data.HEADLINE: Dad Fuel: High-Protein Grilling Menu
data.SUBTITLE: Celebrate Dad with a cookout that's as nutritious as it is delicious
data.FEATURE1_DESC: 450 cal, 42g protein, 28g fat. Grill to medium-rare for maximum flavor
data.FEATURE2_DESC: 40 cal, 4g protein, 4g fiber. Drizzle with olive oil and lemon
data.FEATURE3_DESC: 180 cal, 4g protein, 41g complex carbs. Seasoned with paprika and garlic

data.HEADLINE: Intermittent Fasting in Summer: Good Idea?
data.BODY: IF can work year-round, but summer heat and longer active days mean you need to be smarter about your eating window. Don't fast through your most active hours.
data.STAT_LABEL: Most popular fasting protocol (16hr fast, 8hr eat)
data.TIP_TEXT: Shift your window earlier (10am-6pm) to fuel daytime activities. Stay hydrated during fasting hours — add electrolytes. Break your fast with protein, not carbs. NourishAI tracks your fasting window.

data.HEADLINE: Marathon Training: Nutrition Makes or Breaks You
data.BEFORE_STAT1: Hit the wall at mile 18 — bonked hard
data.BEFORE_STAT2: Carb loading = eating pizza the night before

data.HEADLINE: Longest Day of the Year: Maximize Your Fuel
data.BODY: More daylight means more opportunities to move, train, and burn calories. Make sure your nutrition matches your increased activity level. Summer is not the time to under-eat.
data.STAT_LABEL: of daylight on the summer solstice
data.TIP_TEXT: Eat 5-6 smaller meals spread across waking hours. Include a mid-morning and mid-afternoon snack. Keep cut fruit and veggie sticks prepped in the fridge for grab-and-go fuel.

data.HEADLINE: Collagen Supplements: Worth the Hype?
data.BODY: Collagen is the most abundant protein in your body, supporting joints, skin, and connective tissue. Research shows 10-15g daily may reduce joint pain and improve skin elasticity.
data.STAT_LABEL: Daily collagen for joint and skin benefits
data.TIP_TEXT: Supplement: collagen peptides in coffee or smoothies (flavorless). Whole foods: bone broth (10g per cup), chicken skin, sardines. Pair with Vitamin C to boost absorption.

data.HEADLINE: June Nutrition Wins to Celebrate
data.BODY: Summer is officially here and you've been showing up. Whether you nailed your protein goals, stayed hydrated, or just logged consistently — every day of data is a win.
data.STAT_LABEL: Effort is the only metric that matters
data.TIP_TEXT: Review your June data in NourishAI's Progress tab. Pick ONE macro to focus on improving in July. Small, consistent improvements compound into life-changing results.
